Best model Italian Grand Prix lap 30 predicted vs actual lap time: nbeats (16 drivers)
Driver DriverNumber LapNumber actual_lap_time_s predicted_lap_time_s prediction_gap_s
0 VER 1 30 82.928 82.818 -0.110
1 NOR 4 30 82.639 82.782 0.143
2 BOR 5 30 83.343 82.939 -0.404
3 HAD 6 30 83.701 83.409 -0.292
4 GAS 10 30 83.751 83.894 0.143
5 ANT 12 30 82.775 83.407 0.632
6 LEC 16 30 82.972 82.721 -0.251
7 STR 18 30 83.585 83.658 0.073
8 TSU 22 30 83.732 83.999 0.267
9 ALB 23 30 83.129 83.134 0.005
10 LAW 30 30 83.690 84.037 0.347
11 OCO 31 30 83.632 83.650 0.018
12 HAM 44 30 83.463 83.022 -0.441
13 RUS 63 30 82.601 82.497 -0.104
14 PIA 81 30 82.920 82.640 -0.280
15 BEA 87 30 83.220 83.373 0.153

Now I have listed the information about all drivers on laps 30 to see the results from the model. The first thing that is important there are only 16 drivers this mean that the for the drivers like Alonso who retire from the race at lap 24 and Hulkenberg who did not start at all the model does not make even prediction, which is good and it does make halusination for drivers who did not do the full race also it does not make prediction if you are lapping in the lap 30 if you are pitting it or out at that lap,as we wanted because usally these lap has big difference because they start the next lap techinaly from the pit stationary for around 3 sec and aroun 20 more second until exit the pit lane area with speed limits of 60-80 km/h depending on the track. The drivers that stand out in the predition most are the drivers Bortoleto Hamilton and Antonelli. The driver being way more off is Antonelli with 0.6 sec, but looking at the report for lap by lap report by the formiula one she just had came out of the box and in my opinion he just had not warm his tyre fully yet, which does not give him the fully tire grip yet https://www.formula1.com/en/latest/article/live-coverage-formula-1-pirelli-gran-premio-d-italia-2025.7ba5jM1GQSwpWLnuFAvTnJ. But for Hamilton and Bortoleto I cannot find justified information, so later on in the notebook I will list sum each drivers abs error for the entire race and we will see if it is just one case where the model way a bit bad or is it like this for this driver for the entire races due to their data records limitiation.

In [19]:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

def plot_loss_curves_for_model(model_name: str):
    if model_name not in sequence_model_artifacts:
        print(f"{model_name} not found in sequence_model_artifacts")
        return

    hist = sequence_model_artifacts[model_name].get("loss_history_df")[1:]
    if hist is None or hist.empty:
        print(f"No loss history saved for {model_name}")
        return

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 5))

    if "train_loss" in hist.columns:
        ax.plot(hist["epoch"], hist["train_loss"], marker="o", label="Train Loss")

    if "val_loss" in hist.columns:
        ax.plot(hist["epoch"], hist["val_loss"], marker="o", label="Validation Loss")

    ax.set_title(f"{model_name} Training vs Validation Loss")
    ax.set_xlabel("Epoch")
    ax.set_ylabel("Loss")
    ax.legend()
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()


for model_name in sequence_results_by_model.keys():
    plot_loss_curves_for_model(model_name)

The threee diagrams in summary shows that N-Neats achives the best overall test performance with the lowest lap time Mae of 0.238 sec, which is the best of the rest. Unfortunatly the when we plot the training and valudation lost the model tends to fall apart in the middle of the traiing period which make it to overfit. The best model in my opnion is the TFT model. It is extriamly close to the N-Beats it is only 0.0150 sec slower on average, but contraditory to the N-Beat the learning curve is way smoother and it perform the best without any over or underfitting signals. Nhits also look very balanced but the problem with it is that it is on average 50% worst on performance comapre to the rest. In my domain 0.5 sec it a bit on the bare minimum limit. Just to put it in perspective such gap during qualifying can make the difference of beinig 1 and 10 on the grid.

In [26]:
MODEL_TO_USE = best_model_name if "best_model_name" in globals() else "tft"
DRIVER_TO_USE = "VER"   # Max Verstappen
SOURCE_DF = test_race_df.copy()  # full target race in your current split


def get_saved_model_and_dataset(model_name: str):
    if (
        "sequence_model_artifacts" in globals()
        and model_name in sequence_model_artifacts
        and "best_model" in sequence_model_artifacts[model_name]
        and "train_dataset" in sequence_model_artifacts[model_name]
    ):
        return (
            sequence_model_artifacts[model_name]["best_model"],
            sequence_model_artifacts[model_name]["train_dataset"],
            sequence_model_artifacts[model_name].get("best_model_path", ""),
        )

    saved_results_lookup = None
    if "results_df" in globals() and isinstance(results_df, pd.DataFrame) and not results_df.empty:
        saved_results_lookup = results_df.copy()

    return resolve_model_artifact(
        model_name,
        feature_lists,
        saved_results_lookup=saved_results_lookup,
    )


def build_driver_window_for_any_lap(driver_df: pd.DataFrame, predict_lap_local: int) -> pd.DataFrame:
    work = driver_df.copy()

    if "time_idx" in work.columns:
        work = work.sort_values(["time_idx", "LapNumber"])
    else:
        work = work.sort_values("LapNumber")

    work = (
        work.drop_duplicates(subset=["LapNumber"], keep="last")
        .sort_values("LapNumber")
        .reset_index(drop=True)
    )

    if not (work["LapNumber"] == predict_lap_local).any():
        raise ValueError(f"LapNumber {predict_lap_local} is not present for this driver.")

    raw_required_len_local = MAX_ENCODER_LENGTH + MAX_PREDICTION_LENGTH + 1

    available_until_local = work[work["LapNumber"] <= predict_lap_local].copy()
    window_df_local = available_until_local.tail(raw_required_len_local).copy()

    if len(window_df_local) < raw_required_len_local:
        raise ValueError(
            f"Not enough context up to lap {predict_lap_local}. "
            f"Have {len(window_df_local)} rows, need {raw_required_len_local}."
        )

    _, window_filled_local, _ = fill_sequence_missing_values(
        train_race_df,
        window_df_local,
        window_df_local,
        feature_lists,
    )

    return window_filled_local


driver_event_df = SOURCE_DF[
    SOURCE_DF["Driver"].astype(str).str.upper() == DRIVER_TO_USE
].copy()

if driver_event_df.empty:
    available_drivers = sorted(SOURCE_DF["Driver"].dropna().astype(str).unique().tolist())
    raise ValueError(
        f"No rows found for driver {DRIVER_TO_USE!r}. "
        f"Available drivers in SOURCE_DF: {available_drivers}"
    )

driver_event_df = driver_event_df.sort_values(["LapNumber"]).copy()

target_year_local = int(driver_event_df["Year"].iloc[0])
target_round_local = int(driver_event_df["Round"].iloc[0])
target_event_name_local = str(driver_event_df["EventName"].iloc[0])

best_model_local, train_dataset_local, ckpt_path = get_saved_model_and_dataset(MODEL_TO_USE)

available_lap_numbers = sorted(driver_event_df["LapNumber"].dropna().astype(int).unique().tolist())

comparison_rows_driver = []

for lap_no in available_lap_numbers:
    try:
        window_filled_local = build_driver_window_for_any_lap(driver_event_df, lap_no)
    except ValueError:
        # early laps are expected to fail because there is not enough encoder history yet
        continue

    infer_dataset_local = TimeSeriesDataSet.from_dataset(
        train_dataset_local,
        window_filled_local,
        stop_randomization=True,
    )
    infer_loader_local = infer_dataset_local.to_dataloader(
        train=False,
        batch_size=BATCH_SIZE,
        num_workers=NUM_WORKERS,
    )

    pred_local = best_model_local.predict(infer_loader_local).detach().cpu().reshape(-1).numpy()
    pred_delta_s = float(pred_local[0]) if len(pred_local) else float("nan")

    current_row = window_filled_local.loc[
        window_filled_local["LapNumber"] == int(lap_no)
    ].copy()

    prev_lap_time_s = float(current_row["prev_lap_time_s"].mean())
    actual_lap_time_s = float(current_row["LapTime_s"].mean())
    pred_lap_time_s = prev_lap_time_s + pred_delta_s
    residual_s = pred_lap_time_s - actual_lap_time_s

    comparison_rows_driver.append({
        "model": MODEL_TO_USE,
        "driver": DRIVER_TO_USE,
        "year": target_year_local,
        "round": target_round_local,
        "event_name": target_event_name_local,
        "lap": int(lap_no),
        "prev_lap_time_s": prev_lap_time_s,
        "pred_delta_s": pred_delta_s,
        "pred_lap_time_s": pred_lap_time_s,
        "actual_lap_time_s": actual_lap_time_s,
        "residual_s": residual_s,
        "abs_error_s": abs(residual_s),
    })

driver_full_race_predictions = pd.DataFrame(comparison_rows_driver)

if driver_full_race_predictions.empty:
    raise ValueError(
        "No full-race predictions were generated. "
        "This usually means the driver does not have enough eligible laps after encoder-length filtering."
    )

driver_full_race_predictions = driver_full_race_predictions.sort_values("lap").reset_index(drop=True)

mean_signed_error_s = float(driver_full_race_predictions["residual_s"].mean())
median_signed_error_s = float(driver_full_race_predictions["residual_s"].median())
mae_s = float(driver_full_race_predictions["abs_error_s"].mean())
rmse_s = float(np.sqrt(np.mean(np.square(driver_full_race_predictions["residual_s"]))))

over_count = int((driver_full_race_predictions["residual_s"] > 0).sum())
under_count = int((driver_full_race_predictions["residual_s"] < 0).sum())
equal_count = int((driver_full_race_predictions["residual_s"] == 0).sum())
n_rows = len(driver_full_race_predictions)

if mean_signed_error_s > 0.05:
    bias_label = "tends to OVERPREDICT lap times"
elif mean_signed_error_s < -0.05:
    bias_label = "tends to UNDERPREDICT lap times"
else:
    bias_label = "is roughly BALANCED on average"

driver_bias_summary = pd.DataFrame([{
    "model": MODEL_TO_USE,
    "driver": DRIVER_TO_USE,
    "event_name": target_event_name_local,
    "predicted_laps": n_rows,
    "mae_s": mae_s,
    "rmse_s": rmse_s,
    "mean_signed_error_s": mean_signed_error_s,
    "median_signed_error_s": median_signed_error_s,
    "overpredicted_laps": over_count,
    "underpredicted_laps": under_count,
    "equal_laps": equal_count,
    "overpredicted_pct": 100.0 * over_count / n_rows,
    "underpredicted_pct": 100.0 * under_count / n_rows,
    "bias_label": bias_label,
}])

print(f"Driver-level full-race prediction for {DRIVER_TO_USE} using {MODEL_TO_USE}")
display(driver_bias_summary)
display(driver_full_race_predictions)

32 tft VER 2025 16 Italian Grand Prix 53 81.003 -0.121744 80.881256 81.100 -0.218744 0.218744

By the next table my goal is see how the model performe during the entire race prediction. For this I have purposely have selected Max Verstappend as he is the best performing driver winning the last 4 drivers championships consecatively. What be can obsever most of the laps are in 0.1 to 0.2 seconds off the real time. The two laps that realy out stands are lap 39 https://www.formula1.com/en/latest/article/live-coverage-formula-1-pirelli-gran-premio-d-italia-2025.7ba5jM1GQSwpWLnuFAvTnJ as we can see from the same website he pitted on lap 38 for fresh tyre. Then the upcoming lap is 1.3 second of which is very bad but the reason why it most likely happen to be like that because she use hard tyre, which on there own need usually more tyre to be bring up to temerature to get good grip out of them similarly to what happend to Antonelli a bit earlier. But besides that the other two laps that are a bit off are 51 and 52 and for that I will create other statistic and explain you what happened.

In [27]:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
ax.plot(
    driver_full_race_predictions["lap"],
    driver_full_race_predictions["actual_lap_time_s"],
    marker="o",
    label="Actual lap time",
)
ax.plot(
    driver_full_race_predictions["lap"],
    driver_full_race_predictions["pred_lap_time_s"],
    marker="o",
    label="Predicted lap time",
)
ax.set_title(f"{MODEL_TO_USE} - {DRIVER_TO_USE} full-race lap prediction")
ax.set_xlabel("Lap")
ax.set_ylabel("Lap time (s)")
ax.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))
ax.axhline(0.0, linestyle="--")
ax.plot(
    driver_full_race_predictions["lap"],
    driver_full_race_predictions["residual_s"],
    marker="o",
)
ax.set_title(f"{MODEL_TO_USE} - {DRIVER_TO_USE} residuals by lap")
ax.set_xlabel("Lap")
ax.set_ylabel("Residual (predicted - actual, s)")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

As you can see from the first the lap 51 and 52 the model go very of the result on lap 51 the driver slower his pace by half a second compareded to the previous lap. The reason why it that is because the next lap Max Verstapen, most likely save engery from previous lap and deploy for lap 52where he tried to set the fastest time for the race. I will put link to verify this infonformation from the offical data https://www.formula1.com/en/results/2025/races/1268/italy/fastest-laps. The the most intersting part it that the model chacked that and predict almost perfectly the very similar time for the 51 in lap 52 and later after his attempt it acculy manages to adjust it self back to normal prediction which prof even feature that the model works very well.

In [ ]:
# Full-race lap-time prediction for ALL drivers in the selected test race
# using one already-trained saved model, without retraining.

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from pytorch_forecasting import TimeSeriesDataSet

MODEL_TO_USE = best_model_name if "best_model_name" in globals() else "nbeats"
SOURCE_DF = test_race_df.copy()   # should be the target race test split


def get_saved_model_and_dataset(model_name: str):
    if (
        "sequence_model_artifacts" in globals()
        and model_name in sequence_model_artifacts
        and "best_model" in sequence_model_artifacts[model_name]
        and "train_dataset" in sequence_model_artifacts[model_name]
    ):
        return (
            sequence_model_artifacts[model_name]["best_model"],
            sequence_model_artifacts[model_name]["train_dataset"],
            sequence_model_artifacts[model_name].get("best_model_path", ""),
        )

    saved_results_lookup = None
    if "results_df" in globals() and isinstance(results_df, pd.DataFrame) and not results_df.empty:
        saved_results_lookup = results_df.copy()

    return resolve_model_artifact(
        model_name,
        feature_lists,
        saved_results_lookup=saved_results_lookup,
    )


def build_driver_window_for_any_lap(driver_df: pd.DataFrame, predict_lap_local: int) -> pd.DataFrame:
    work = driver_df.copy()

    if "time_idx" in work.columns:
        work = work.sort_values(["time_idx", "LapNumber"])
    else:
        work = work.sort_values("LapNumber")

    work = (
        work.drop_duplicates(subset=["LapNumber"], keep="last")
        .sort_values("LapNumber")
        .reset_index(drop=True)
    )

    if not (work["LapNumber"] == predict_lap_local).any():
        raise ValueError(f"LapNumber {predict_lap_local} is not present for this driver.")

    raw_required_len_local = MAX_ENCODER_LENGTH + MAX_PREDICTION_LENGTH + 1

    available_until_local = work[work["LapNumber"] <= predict_lap_local].copy()
    window_df_local = available_until_local.tail(raw_required_len_local).copy()

    if len(window_df_local) < raw_required_len_local:
        raise ValueError(
            f"Not enough context up to lap {predict_lap_local}. "
            f"Have {len(window_df_local)} rows, need {raw_required_len_local}."
        )

    _, window_filled_local, _ = fill_sequence_missing_values(
        train_race_df,
        window_df_local,
        window_df_local,
        feature_lists,
    )

    return window_filled_local


if SOURCE_DF.empty:
    raise ValueError("SOURCE_DF is empty.")

if "selected_target_event_id" in globals() and "event_id" in SOURCE_DF.columns:
    source_event_df = SOURCE_DF[SOURCE_DF["event_id"] == selected_target_event_id].copy()
    if source_event_df.empty:
        source_event_df = SOURCE_DF.copy()
else:
    source_event_df = SOURCE_DF.copy()

if source_event_df.empty:
    raise ValueError("No rows found for the selected event.")

target_year_local = int(source_event_df["Year"].iloc[0])
target_round_local = int(source_event_df["Round"].iloc[0])
target_event_name_local = str(source_event_df["EventName"].iloc[0])

best_model_local, train_dataset_local, ckpt_path = get_saved_model_and_dataset(MODEL_TO_USE)

all_drivers = sorted(source_event_df["Driver"].dropna().astype(str).unique().tolist())
comparison_rows_all_drivers = []

for driver_code in all_drivers:
    driver_event_df = source_event_df[
        source_event_df["Driver"].astype(str).str.upper() == driver_code
    ].copy()

    if driver_event_df.empty:
        continue

    driver_event_df = driver_event_df.sort_values("LapNumber").copy()
    available_lap_numbers = sorted(driver_event_df["LapNumber"].dropna().astype(int).unique().tolist())

    for lap_no in available_lap_numbers:
        try:
            window_filled_local = build_driver_window_for_any_lap(driver_event_df, lap_no)
        except ValueError:
            # early laps are expected to fail because there is not enough encoder history yet
            continue

        infer_dataset_local = TimeSeriesDataSet.from_dataset(
            train_dataset_local,
            window_filled_local,
            stop_randomization=True,
        )
        infer_loader_local = infer_dataset_local.to_dataloader(
            train=False,
            batch_size=BATCH_SIZE,
            num_workers=NUM_WORKERS,
        )

        pred_local = best_model_local.predict(infer_loader_local).detach().cpu().reshape(-1).numpy()
        pred_delta_s = float(pred_local[0]) if len(pred_local) else float("nan")

        current_row = window_filled_local.loc[
            window_filled_local["LapNumber"] == int(lap_no)
        ].copy()

        prev_lap_time_s = float(current_row["prev_lap_time_s"].mean())
        actual_lap_time_s = float(current_row["LapTime_s"].mean())
        pred_lap_time_s = prev_lap_time_s + pred_delta_s
        residual_s = pred_lap_time_s - actual_lap_time_s

        comparison_rows_all_drivers.append({
            "model": MODEL_TO_USE,
            "driver": driver_code,
            "year": target_year_local,
            "round": target_round_local,
            "event_name": target_event_name_local,
            "lap": int(lap_no),
            "prev_lap_time_s": prev_lap_time_s,
            "pred_delta_s": pred_delta_s,
            "pred_lap_time_s": pred_lap_time_s,
            "actual_lap_time_s": actual_lap_time_s,
            "residual_s": residual_s,
            "abs_error_s": abs(residual_s),
        })

all_drivers_full_race_predictions = pd.DataFrame(comparison_rows_all_drivers)

if all_drivers_full_race_predictions.empty:
    raise ValueError(
        "No full-race predictions were generated for any driver. "
        "This usually means there is not enough encoder context."
    )

all_drivers_full_race_predictions = (
    all_drivers_full_race_predictions
    .sort_values(["driver", "lap"])
    .reset_index(drop=True)
)

driver_race_error_summary = (
    all_drivers_full_race_predictions
    .groupby("driver", as_index=False)
    .agg(
        predicted_laps=("lap", "count"),
        sum_abs_error_s=("abs_error_s", "sum"),
        avg_abs_error_s=("abs_error_s", "mean"),
        rmse_s=("residual_s", lambda x: float(np.sqrt(np.mean(np.square(x))))),
        mean_signed_error_s=("residual_s", "mean"),
        median_signed_error_s=("residual_s", "median"),
        best_abs_error_s=("abs_error_s", "min"),
        worst_abs_error_s=("abs_error_s", "max"),
    )
    .sort_values(["avg_abs_error_s", "sum_abs_error_s"], ascending=[True, True])
    .reset_index(drop=True)
)

driver_race_error_summary["bias_label"] = np.where(
    driver_race_error_summary["mean_signed_error_s"] > 0.05,
    "overpredict",
    np.where(
        driver_race_error_summary["mean_signed_error_s"] < -0.05,
        "underpredict",
        "roughly_balanced",
    )
)

print(f"Full-race driver summary using model {MODEL_TO_USE} for {target_event_name_local} ({target_year_local} R{target_round_local})")
display(driver_race_error_summary)

Full-race driver summary using model nbeats for Italian Grand Prix (2025 R16)
driver predicted_laps sum_abs_error_s avg_abs_error_s rmse_s mean_signed_error_s median_signed_error_s best_abs_error_s worst_abs_error_s bias_label

9 STR 31 9.884601 0.318858 0.424472 -0.007172 0.005203 0.000610 0.986229 roughly_balanced
10 BOR 32 10.281825 0.321307 0.421539 -0.039380 0.024735 0.009611 1.352442 roughly_balanced
11 NOR 33 10.712685 0.324627 0.485487 -0.021808 -0.049963 0.010574 1.783567 roughly_balanced
12 GAS 28 10.338780 0.369242 0.704000 -0.051775 0.097991 0.010399 2.328890 underpredict
13 SAI 30 11.218439 0.373948 0.526200 -0.059096 0.013626 0.015041 1.311292 underpredict
14 PIA 33 12.941796 0.392176 0.604471 -0.093513 -0.062918 0.028682 1.668269 underpredict
15 TSU 32 13.013744 0.406680 0.589141 -0.049127 0.043888 0.002025 1.568589 roughly_balanced
16 ANT 30 12.270364 0.409012 0.595665 -0.108132 -0.013748 0.004547 1.483431 underpredict
17 LAW 32 14.000721 0.437523 0.611724 -0.126402 -0.035736 0.001651 1.680905 underpredict
18 COL 29 16.570034 0.571380 0.867379 -0.143091 -0.091801 0.027642 2.331508 underpredict

I have created this table to show each drivers values from the best model for their predion for all lap times during the race. The best driver that predict most accurate is being Ocon with total error for the course of the entire race time 4.5 seconds with average lap time of 0.14, which perfect mean value close to 0 which it is very balanced in it predition.The think that I am most supprised if how good it predic the lap time of Alonso, which drive only 24 (we predict from lap 20) laps and most likely have some technical problems or crash and retire from the race you can see the offical results here https://www.formula1.com/en/results/2025/races/1268/italy/race-result and that explaing showing only 19 driver becasue Hulkeberd did not start at all.

Another thing that if also noticable is last few drivers with the worst prediction of all that being Antonelli, Lawson and Colopinto the reason these driver prediction is the 'worst' compare to the others is the fact that the 2025 was the year of the rokies(new driver in the sport) and for these partical drivers they dont have any information in the previous year. Something even more spefic about Anotonelli is her is also driving for Merceder a team that is in 3-4 during that session and it replacing Hamilton seat, which win 7 WDC so the model is more or less trying to make him perform as well as he does in the previous each, but on the other side the his teammate Russell, which has been racing for this team all the 3 years used in the dataset got the 4 best prediction. The same thing can be said for Bearman which is the teammate of Ocon and just for the same reason he is rank a bit lower.

The other thing that suprice me is how it predict the Mclaren driver Norris and Piastri despite being the fastest team for the overall season, just becasue they had improve way more the car from 2023-2025 from 4 fastest to 1 fastest car some times the model on can go up to 1.7 sec off in the worst cases.

Other case there the gap between teammate is to big is RedBull being the worst one in between the team mates, where Tsunoda is on average 0.390 sec of the actual predion almost 2 times Verstapen. And the model is trying to push Tsunoda more of less close to Max times. The reason why this is happing is that for the past 3 years RebBull is building the car entirely in Verstappen style of driving and this teammates have hard time maching his times during race and they cannot someone to driver that perform even close to him, so they change his teammate very offen I will send link if you are interested in this topic. https://www.tsn.ca/motorsports/formula-1/article/being-maxs-teammate-a-timeline-of-red-bull-no-2-failures-n1-43060520/

In [24]:
race_all_drivers_error_summary = pd.DataFrame([{
    "predicted_laps_total": int(len(all_drivers_full_race_predictions)),
    "total_actual_race_time_s": float(all_drivers_full_race_predictions["actual_lap_time_s"].sum()),
    "total_predicted_race_time_s": float(all_drivers_full_race_predictions["pred_lap_time_s"].sum()),
    "total_signed_error_s": float(all_drivers_full_race_predictions["residual_s"].sum()),
    "avg_signed_error_s": float(all_drivers_full_race_predictions["residual_s"].mean()),
    "total_abs_error_s": float(all_drivers_full_race_predictions["abs_error_s"].sum()),
    "avg_abs_error_s": float(all_drivers_full_race_predictions["abs_error_s"].mean()),
}])

race_all_drivers_error_summary["total_race_time_error_pct"] = (
    (race_all_drivers_error_summary["total_actual_race_time_s"] - race_all_drivers_error_summary["total_predicted_race_time_s"]).abs()
    / race_all_drivers_error_summary["total_actual_race_time_s"]
    * 100.0
)

race_all_drivers_error_summary["total_race_time_closeness_pct"] = (
    100.0 - race_all_drivers_error_summary["total_race_time_error_pct"]
)

display(race_all_drivers_error_summary)
predicted_laps_total total_actual_race_time_s total_predicted_race_time_s total_signed_error_s avg_signed_error_s total_abs_error_s avg_abs_error_s total_race_time_error_pct total_race_time_closeness_pct
0 570 47307.898 47277.328718 -30.569282 -0.05363 182.203888 0.319656 0.064618 99.935382

Beased of these result the models is 0.319 sec off per lap for each driver for the entire race.This is considerate small gap and with in 0.3 sec which it deam the bare mininmus for this domain to count it as use full model. With total of time for the entire race being 182 which is still very good number because in this case we got 18 drivers which is fine given the over all race duration of 47307 seconds. Based of the total signed error we can also state that our model in negative values meaning that it predictied slightly fasters laps overalll than what they really are.

Conclussion¶

I deam this model very good based on what I start at the beginning of this project with models with gap of 9 seconds which is a lot as I explaing a bit earlier. After very carefully data reading and filtering the most improtant feature going to telemetry data which in my opinion is like 50% off the successful prediction because it record every driver movement to the car 10 times every 1 sec. I think I have applied the best possible tactics like rolling window to get every bit of the performance. I also believe this is just still mediam level model, if this project get up scale to the entire race calendar of 24 races how it was planned to do, it will learn even better the pattern like Verstappen trying to put the fastest lap at the end of the race, and will have more data for referances and the overall the MAE in my opnion will go down to at least 0.150- 0.200 sec per lap. For the use of this model, I believe such a project will be more in use for the actual racing teams like Ferrari, Red Bull and Mercedes, or people that are really into every information of the sport not the casual user. The reason why I am saying that is,because for the normal user they can use such a model to simulate races before the actual race to understnad what result to expect from their favorite driver, but to make it real this sport is every driven by unexpected event like crashes though the beginning middle and the end of the races with mixes of unexpected weather condition, it will be harding to take them into account before the actual race happpens. On the other hand if someone from the team like race enginner use such a model it can help them like a tool to determ, how the drivers is performing live time with hte needed computing power, because such a model should be rerun every lap to give proper result and base of the result to help them to make the best race strategy like, pitting earlier if they see that their time slow down compare to the competitor, to keep their position if they are being chased.
